summary: A monster 1974 second‑set jam (He's Gone > Truckin' > Jam > The Other One > Space > Stella) and a legendary China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, captured in a clear, well‑balanced recording.
review: Reviewers rave about the incendiary C > R sequence, Phil Lesh’s “man‑possessed” bass work, and the sprawling second‑set jam that includes the only known slide‑guitar moment on He's Gone. The first set is praised as tight and energetic, with highlights like Half‑Step > Roses and a fiery Big River. No recording flaws are mentioned, suggesting a clean source that lets the performance shine.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are highly positive (4‑5 stars) and focus on exceptional musical moments; recording quality is not criticized, implying a good source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific technical complaints; reviewers imply the audio is clear enough to appreciate nuanced performances.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as extremely well‑played and energetic; second set features the classic 1974 monster jam that ties the show together.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Unique, slide‑guitar moment on He's Gone and a standout C > R jam praised by reviewers.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “grooving” and playing like a man possessed; bass work highlighted throughout.
